paper_title: The role of nutraceutical medications in men with non bacterial chronic prostatitis and chronic pelvic pain syndrome: A prospective non blinded study utilizing flower pollen extracts versus bioflavonoids.

methods.description: A phase I-II study conducted to evaluate the effects of flower pollen extract and quercetin on patients with CP/CPPS, following ethical guidelines and clinical trial standards.

steps.description: The study was conducted according to Good Clinical Practice guidelines and the ethical principles of the Declaration of Helsinki.
steps.searchable_summary: ensure study follows ethical guidelines and clinical practice standards

steps.description: Between March 2016 to June 2016, all consecutive patients presenting with clinical diagnosis of CP/CPPS were recruited.
All eligible patients completed and returned base questionnaires (IPSS, QoL, NIH-CPSI).
Every patient underwent a thorough urological examination and Meares-Stamey test.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct urological examination and Meares-Stamey test

steps.description: Patients were randomized to receive either flower pollen extract or quercetin for 4 weeks.
A telephone follow-up was conducted 10 days after treatment to ensure compliance.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct follow-up to ensure treatment compliance

steps.description: An outpatient follow-up was carried out 30 days after treatment with new questionnaires and urological examination.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct follow-up with questionnaires and examination

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data from the clinical study, including baseline comparisons and treatment effects.

steps.description: The main statistical analysis was performed on the patient population 'Intent-To-Treat'.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical analysis on Intent-To-Treat population

steps.description: Baseline patient characteristics are reported as mean, SD, median, IQR, frequency, or relative percentage.
For baseline comparison in the two arms, T-test for independent variables and chi-square for proportions were used.
steps.searchable_summary: perform T-test and chi-square for baseline comparison

steps.description: Comparison of NIH-CPSI index between baseline and 30 days was done using paired T-test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform paired T-test on NIH-CPSI index

steps.description: Sample size was calculated from a starting average waiting time of NIH-CPSI variation.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate sample size based on NIH-CPSI variation
